description: Documented low-coverage files in apps/server/src/ with rationale

apps/server/src/routes/auth.ts.rationale: Password change route requires complex Elysia context + auth middleware setup. Core logic (status endpoint) is fully tested via direct service-level tests (auth/password.ts). Route integration can be tested end-to-end via the full app context, which is covered by integration tests.
gets_tested_via: full app integration in bootstrap tests

apps/server/src/routes/snapshots.ts.rationale: Rollback error handling path (lines 69-86) requires orchestrating a pipeline failure. Happy-path rollback is fully tested. Error handling is integration-tested via pipeline tests.

apps/server/src/server-bootstrap.ts.rationale: Bootstrap/wiring code for different transport modes (SSH, LocalFs, InMemory) and multi-step initialization. Tested indirectly via app context creation in route tests. Transport-specific paths (SSH setup, remote config validation) are covered by transport-layer tests. Hard to unit test in isolation because it's glue code with many conditional branches.

apps/server/src/transport/ssh-adapter.ts.rationale: Low-level SSH primitives (ssh2.Client wrapping) with complex async lifecycle, socket management, and error states. Realistic testing requires a live SSH server or extensive mocking of the ssh2 library. High-level SSH transport tests cover the adapter indirectly. SSH deployment is manually tested in production.

notes: Threshold is ≥80% for overall apps/server/src/ coverage — achieved at 91.17%.
Low-coverage files are either integration/bootstrap code or infrastructure (SSH/transport) that are hard to isolate.
These files ARE tested, but via indirect paths (e.g., route tests use wireApp() which exercises server-bootstrap.ts).
SSH adapter and server-bootstrap are load-bearing infrastructure with high test leverage — improving isolated unit coverage has diminishing returns.
